# Why your washing machine will soon have to run during the day

[Podcast](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/in-spraak/podcast/index.html)

[Energy](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/energie/index.html)

08/10/2025 • 2 minutes reading

[Albert Jan Swart](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/albert-jan-swart/index.html)

The Dutch power grid is creaking at the seams. To combat grid congestion, the Netherlands must invest tens of billions. How did it get to this point? Journalist Esther van Rijswijk discusses this in this episode of the podcast series Achter de Cijfers with sector economist Albert Jan Swart.

Electric cooking, driving and heating ensure an increase in our electricity consumption, while solar panels and wind turbines provide more and more energy. The result? Peak moments when demand and supply are too high at the same time. This phenomenon is called grid congestion and now leads to new homes or businesses no longer being connected. Albert Jan Swart, sector economist at ABN AMRO, talks in this podcast about what lies behind the problems on our electricity grid. From the historical structure of our energy network to the billions of investments that are now required - and the role that our own behavior can play. Because maybe moving some laundry or loading your car is more important than you think.

## What to do about grid congestion?

## More information

A number of significant steps have been taken in the past year to reduce the consequences of grid congestion. For example, the new Energy Act has been adopted and the ACM has already adjusted many policy rules to give companies more room to use the scarce capacity on the grid in a smarter way. In the report [Grid Congestion: What's New](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/alle-sectoren/netcongestie-voor-wat-hoort-wat.html), ABN AMRO explains what entrepreneurs can do to quickly use more electricity despite grid congestion.

## Podcast Behind the Figures

Curious about what's going on behind the biggest economic developments? In the podcast [Achter de Cijfers](https://www.abnamro.com/nl/podcast-achter-de-figures) we take you through a current topic every week, together with experts. From unaffordable housing to rising inflation, we explain it clearly and without jargon. Because economics is more than figures and graphs, it's about your wallet. Behind the Numbers is the economics teacher you always wanted.

## In conversation

Our experts and representatives will discuss current topics with entrepreneurs and others on various stages and platforms. They talk about matters such as sustainable entrepreneurship, circular economy, real estate and social impact. Follow the webinars, podcasts and interviews.

[Watch the videos or listen to the podcasts](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/in-spraak/index.html)

## Read more in the energy sector

The energy sector is rapidly evolving as the energy transition continues to gain momentum. ABN AMRO wants to play a leading role in accelerating the energy transition, which is full of opportunities and obstacles.
